questions.question: A random variable X has a joint probability distribution given by P(X, Y) = (0.3 + 0.4x) * (0.2 + 0.1y) for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1 and 0 ≤ y ≤ 1. Assuming X and Y are independent, find the marginal probability distributions of X and Y. (5 marks)

A coin is tossed three times. The probability of getting 'heads' in the first and second tosses is 0.6, and the probability of getting 'tails' in the third toss is 0.7. Use Bayes' rule to update the probability of the coin being fair given that it has landed on 'heads' twice in the first three tosses. (5 marks)
Two events A and B are independent if P(A ∩ B) = P(A) * P(B). Prove this statement using the definition of conditional probability. (5 marks)
